item.title: Survive the Backrooms Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
In Survive the Backrooms on Roblox , you’re trapped in the Backrooms and have to survive for 99 days. This Roblox game is all about lasting as long as you can in this mysterious place, and it’s perfect for players who love a bit of thrill without getting too scary.

That’s why the working codes are such a big deal! They give you free rewards right away, like extra items that make surviving way more fun and easier. If you want to get ahead and have a better shot at beating those tough days, grab these working codes fast before they expire. They’re simple to use and can turn your game around in seconds.

All Survive the Backrooms Codes

Working Survive the Backrooms Codes

Code

Reward(s)

SALIMBRADLEY

7 Emeralds

Expired Survive the Backrooms Codes

There are currently no expired Survive the Backrooms codes.

Roblox codes hand you emeralds that you can use inside the game. Emeralds help you grab cool stuff like better tools or extra supplies, which means you don’t have to worry so much about running out of what you need while trying to last those full 99 days.

How to Redeem Survive the Backrooms Codes

  • Open Survive the Backrooms in Roblox .
  • Look on the left side of your screen for the Codes button and click it.
  • Type or paste the code exactly as it shows—no extra spaces!
  • Hit the Claim button and watch your rewards pop up right away.
  • If it works, you’ll see the emeralds added to your account super quick.

How to Get More Survive the Backrooms Codes

The easiest way to stay in the loop is by joining the official Discord server. That’s where the developers share news, hints, and sometimes codes! You can chat with other players, too, and ask questions about surviving the Backrooms.

We update our articles all the time with the newest working codes, including for this Roblox game, so the smartest move is to bookmark this page and check back every few days. That way, you’ll catch every fresh code as soon as it drops and never miss out on free emeralds or other surprises.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
In Survive the Backrooms on Roblox , you’re trapped in the Backrooms and have to survive for 99 days. This Roblox game is all about lasting as long as you can in this mysterious place, and it’s perfect for players who love a bit of thrill without getting too scary.

That’s why the working codes are such a big deal! They give you free rewards right away, like extra items that make surviving way more fun and easier. If you want to get ahead and have a better shot at beating those tough days, grab these working codes fast before they expire. They’re simple to use and can turn your game around in seconds.

All Survive the Backrooms Codes

Working Survive the Backrooms Codes

Code

Reward(s)

SALIMBRADLEY

7 Emeralds

Expired Survive the Backrooms Codes

There are currently no expired Survive the Backrooms codes.

Roblox codes hand you emeralds that you can use inside the game. Emeralds help you grab cool stuff like better tools or extra supplies, which means you don’t have to worry so much about running out of what you need while trying to last those full 99 days.

How to Redeem Survive the Backrooms Codes

  • Open Survive the Backrooms in Roblox .
  • Look on the left side of your screen for the Codes button and click it.
  • Type or paste the code exactly as it shows—no extra spaces!
  • Hit the Claim button and watch your rewards pop up right away.
  • If it works, you’ll see the emeralds added to your account super quick.

How to Get More Survive the Backrooms Codes

The easiest way to stay in the loop is by joining the official Discord server. That’s where the developers share news, hints, and sometimes codes! You can chat with other players, too, and ask questions about surviving the Backrooms.

We update our articles all the time with the newest working codes, including for this Roblox game, so the smartest move is to bookmark this page and check back every few days. That way, you’ll catch every fresh code as soon as it drops and never miss out on free emeralds or other surprises.

item.title: All The Cauldron North Riddler Puzzle Locations & Solutions in LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight
item.description:
Looking to solve every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North in LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight ? The Cauldron North, which appears on the in-game map as The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals , has seven Riddler Puzzles to find and solve, and several of them require specific characters or abilities before they can be completed.

This guide primarily focuses on puzzle solutions, since Riddler Puzzle icons can be revealed on the map after activating the district tower. Below, you’ll find every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals in LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight , its coordinates, details on any required character or ability, and how to solve it.

For more collectibles and 100% completion help, see our LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Collectibles Guide & Locations. For broader help with missions, characters, unlocks, and Gotham exploration, visit our guide directory.

All The Cauldron North Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzles Overview

There are seven Riddler Puzzles in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals . Some can be solved with just Batman, while others require Robin, Nightwing, Catwoman, or Jim Gordon. You can see in-game coordinates by opening the map, moving the cursor around, and looking in the bottom-right corner of the screen.

Puzzle

Coordinates

Required Character

Short Time Circuit

153, 522

Any character

Need a Breakthrough

146, 538

Nightwing, Batman

The Key-side Conundrum

108, 543

Catwoman

Pipe Down

148, 570

Jim Gordon, Batman

Tight Corner

188, 586

Robin

Birdarandom

216, 567

Robin

Mirror Mirth

Puzzle Room at 138, 512

Any character

What You Need Before Solving Every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North

You can't solve every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals as soon as you reach the district. Several puzzles require later characters or tools, including Robin , Nightwing , and Catwoman . Jim Gordon and Batman’s core tools are also used, but you should already have them by the time you are seriously clearing The Cauldron North.

If you're missing one of those characters or abilities, continue the story and return later. For unlock timing, see our How to Unlock All Playable Characters guide. For more on character tools and upgrades, check our Gadgets & Upgrades Guide.

All The Cauldron North Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzle Solutions

Short Time Circuit Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 153, 522
  • Required Character: Any character

Interact with the large lever to deactivate the two electrified strips flanking the cube. Move a character onto one of those deactivated strips and keep them there.

Switch to your second character and interact with the lever. Quickly switch back to your first character and move them onto the middle strip before the flanking strips are re-electrified.

This puzzle is easier to solve when playing co-op, as you won't have to quickly switch between characters.

Need a Breakthrough Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 146, 538
  • Required Character: Nightwing, Batman

Push the cart in the alcove across from the wall blocking the cube forward until it stops. Use Nightwing's Cable Launcher to connect the object on the cart to the end of the nearby crane.

Now grapple up to the metal walkway above the crane and use Nightwing to send power into the machine you find there. The crane will now swing and break a wall, and you can use Batman's Spray Explosive to break a second wall and access the cube.

The Key-side Conundrum Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 108, 543
  • Required Character: Catwoman

To solve this puzzle, you must collect three keys and use them on three locks that are connected to gates blocking the cube. Here's how to get those keys:

  • Key 1: Start at the locks and look south. You'll see a suspended cage in front of you, and you can use Catwoman's Whip on its screw to release the cage and free a key.
  • Key 2: Descend the small flight of stairs just north of the locks. At the bottom, use Catwoman's Whip on the screw extending from the elevated pipe to release water and interact with the lever to move the pipe and put out a fire. This will reveal a vent you can enter with Catwoman's Call Kitty ability, and there is a key in the area past it.
  • Key 3: There is a cage at the top of the stairs connected to three buttons, and you can follow the wires attached to the cage to find those buttons. Put Batman on one of the buttons on top of the shipping containers, Catwoman on top of the other button on the shipping containers, and the kitten that you can summon with Catwoman's Call Kitty ability on the third button to open the cage.

This is yet another Riddler Puzzle that is easier to solve when playing multiplayer, as getting characters to stay on their buttons solo can be a little finicky.

Pipe Down Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 148, 570
  • Required Character: Jim Gordon, Batman

Start this puzzle by using a Batarang-like projectile to break the valve to the left of the cube. Then use Jim Gordon's Foam Sprayer to stop the water.

Now grapple up to the area to the right of the cube and follow the horizontal pipe to the right. As you travel in that direction, push the pipe on the cart forward. Then use Jim Gordon's Foam Sprayer on the gears when the moving pipe is in the bottom position, and use Batman's Batclaw on the hook to pull a pipe section into place.

Once the horizontal pipe is all in place, follow the vertical pipe over the wall, breaking the suspended box before you fall. At the bottom, use the box's components to complete the pipe and interact with the lever to send water to the cube. That will free the cube, allowing you to collect it.

Tight Corner Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 188, 586
  • Required Character: Robin

You solve this puzzle by hitting three targets that are connected to the cage with the cube. Follow the wires extending from the cage to find the targets, and then perform these actions:

  • Target 1: For the target connected to the wall, use Batman's Batclaw to open the cage.
  • Target 2: The target in the area above the cube is in a small structure, and you must use Robin to pry open its entrance.
  • Target 3: For the final target, go to the water just left of the cube. Use Robin's Cable Launcher on the yellow points connected to the walls to create a tightrope. Stand on it to get a good angle for hitting the target.

Birdarandom Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 216, 567
  • Required Character: Robin

Begin in the area beneath the cube, where you'll find a small battery and a contraption with two purple ovals. Go behind the contraption and push it forward to move the ovals and reveal a battery terminal. Place the small battery into it.

Now position Robin on the oil tank across from the cube and have Batman stand on the button next to the cube. You'll now see three switches above the cube that you can hit with a single projectile from Robin, and you should target them in this order before releasing the projectile: right, middle, left .

As was the case with Short Time Circuit and The Key-side Conundrum, a second player is very helpful for this puzzle.

Mirror Mirth Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: Puzzle Room at 138, 512
  • Required Character: Any character

Mirror Mirth is found inside a Puzzle Room at 138, 512. If you aren't able to enter it, continue solving Riddler Puzzles across Gotham and wait for it to appear on the map.

Subscribe for LEGO Batman guides, puzzles and collectibles

Get deeper Gotham help by joining the newsletter for expanded LEGO Batman walkthroughs and complete collectible guides — including Riddler puzzle solutions, precise map coordinates, character-specific tactics, and step-by-step tips for 100% completion.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Once you can go inside the Puzzle Room, take these actions to solve it:

  1. Grab the lamp on the table and place it on the pedestal furthest from the mirror.
  2. Collect the bear from the bookshelf and place it on the seat in front of the piano.
  3. Press the red button near the entrance to the room to roll up a dock door. Grab the candles from the ground beyond the dock door and put them on the table.
  4. Pick up the crank near the table, take it into the room where you found the candles, use it on the gears to lower the cage with the boombox, and place the boombox on the pedestal closest to the mirror.

Do Towers Reveal The Cauldron North Riddler Puzzles?

Yes , activating The Cauldron North tower will reveal collectible and activity icons in the district, including Riddler Puzzles. However, finding a puzzle is just the beginning, as you must then solve it.

For more on map-reveal towers, see our All Towers & How to Access Them guide.

Do The Cauldron North Riddler Puzzles Count Toward 100% Completion?

Yes , you need to solve all seven The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzles to earn the A Watchful Collector achievement and reach 100% completion.

More LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight Collectible Guides

Looking for more Gotham collectibles and puzzle solutions? Start with these guides:

  • Collectibles Guide & Locations
  • All Towers & How to Access Them
  • Old Gotham South Riddler Puzzle Locations & Solutions
  • Old Gotham South Cluemaster Puzzle Locations & Solutions
  • Old Gotham North Riddler Puzzle Locations & Solutions
  • Old Gotham North Cluemaster Puzzle Locations & Solutions

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight

Follow
Followed
Action
Adventure
Superhero
Open-World
Systems
Released
May 22, 2026
Developer(s)
TT Games
Publisher(s)
Warner Bros. Games
Franchise
LEGO Batman
PC Release Date
May 22, 2026
7 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Superhero, Open-World
Powered by

Looking to solve every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North in LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight ? The Cauldron North, which appears on the in-game map as The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals , has seven Riddler Puzzles to find and solve, and several of them require specific characters or abilities before they can be completed.

This guide primarily focuses on puzzle solutions, since Riddler Puzzle icons can be revealed on the map after activating the district tower. Below, you’ll find every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals in LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight , its coordinates, details on any required character or ability, and how to solve it.

For more collectibles and 100% completion help, see our LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Collectibles Guide & Locations. For broader help with missions, characters, unlocks, and Gotham exploration, visit our guide directory.

All The Cauldron North Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzles Overview

There are seven Riddler Puzzles in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals . Some can be solved with just Batman, while others require Robin, Nightwing, Catwoman, or Jim Gordon. You can see in-game coordinates by opening the map, moving the cursor around, and looking in the bottom-right corner of the screen.

Puzzle

Coordinates

Required Character

Short Time Circuit

153, 522

Any character

Need a Breakthrough

146, 538

Nightwing, Batman

The Key-side Conundrum

108, 543

Catwoman

Pipe Down

148, 570

Jim Gordon, Batman

Tight Corner

188, 586

Robin

Birdarandom

216, 567

Robin

Mirror Mirth

Puzzle Room at 138, 512

Any character

What You Need Before Solving Every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North

You can't solve every Riddler Puzzle in The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals as soon as you reach the district. Several puzzles require later characters or tools, including Robin , Nightwing , and Catwoman . Jim Gordon and Batman’s core tools are also used, but you should already have them by the time you are seriously clearing The Cauldron North.

If you're missing one of those characters or abilities, continue the story and return later. For unlock timing, see our How to Unlock All Playable Characters guide. For more on character tools and upgrades, check our Gadgets & Upgrades Guide.

All The Cauldron North Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzle Solutions

Short Time Circuit Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 153, 522
  • Required Character: Any character

Interact with the large lever to deactivate the two electrified strips flanking the cube. Move a character onto one of those deactivated strips and keep them there.

Switch to your second character and interact with the lever. Quickly switch back to your first character and move them onto the middle strip before the flanking strips are re-electrified.

This puzzle is easier to solve when playing co-op, as you won't have to quickly switch between characters.

Need a Breakthrough Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 146, 538
  • Required Character: Nightwing, Batman

Push the cart in the alcove across from the wall blocking the cube forward until it stops. Use Nightwing's Cable Launcher to connect the object on the cart to the end of the nearby crane.

Now grapple up to the metal walkway above the crane and use Nightwing to send power into the machine you find there. The crane will now swing and break a wall, and you can use Batman's Spray Explosive to break a second wall and access the cube.

The Key-side Conundrum Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 108, 543
  • Required Character: Catwoman

To solve this puzzle, you must collect three keys and use them on three locks that are connected to gates blocking the cube. Here's how to get those keys:

  • Key 1: Start at the locks and look south. You'll see a suspended cage in front of you, and you can use Catwoman's Whip on its screw to release the cage and free a key.
  • Key 2: Descend the small flight of stairs just north of the locks. At the bottom, use Catwoman's Whip on the screw extending from the elevated pipe to release water and interact with the lever to move the pipe and put out a fire. This will reveal a vent you can enter with Catwoman's Call Kitty ability, and there is a key in the area past it.
  • Key 3: There is a cage at the top of the stairs connected to three buttons, and you can follow the wires attached to the cage to find those buttons. Put Batman on one of the buttons on top of the shipping containers, Catwoman on top of the other button on the shipping containers, and the kitten that you can summon with Catwoman's Call Kitty ability on the third button to open the cage.

This is yet another Riddler Puzzle that is easier to solve when playing multiplayer, as getting characters to stay on their buttons solo can be a little finicky.

Pipe Down Riddler Puzzle Solution

  • Coordinates: 148, 570
  • Required Character: Jim Gordon, Batman

Start this puzzle by using a Batarang-like projectile to break the valve to the left of the cube. Then use Jim Gordon's Foam Sprayer to stop the water.

Now grapple up to the area to the right of the cube and follow the horizontal pipe to the right. As you travel in that direction, push the pipe on the cart forward. Then use Jim Gordon's Foam Sprayer on the gears when the moving pipe is in the bottom position, and use Batman's Batclaw on the hook to pull a pipe section into place.

Once the horizontal pipe is all in place, follow the vertical pipe over the wall, breaking the suspended box before you fall. At the bottom, use the box's components to complete the pipe and interact with the lever to send water to the cube. That will free the cube, allowing you to collect it.

  • Coordinates: 188, 586
  • Required Character: Robin

You solve this puzzle by hitting three targets that are connected to the cage with the cube. Follow the wires extending from the cage to find the targets, and then perform these actions:

  • Target 1: For the target connected to the wall, use Batman's Batclaw to open the cage.
  • Target 2: The target in the area above the cube is in a small structure, and you must use Robin to pry open its entrance.
  • Target 3: For the final target, go to the water just left of the cube. Use Robin's Cable Launcher on the yellow points connected to the walls to create a tightrope. Stand on it to get a good angle for hitting the target.

  • Coordinates: 216, 567
  • Required Character: Robin

Begin in the area beneath the cube, where you'll find a small battery and a contraption with two purple ovals. Go behind the contraption and push it forward to move the ovals and reveal a battery terminal. Place the small battery into it.

Now position Robin on the oil tank across from the cube and have Batman stand on the button next to the cube. You'll now see three switches above the cube that you can hit with a single projectile from Robin, and you should target them in this order before releasing the projectile: right, middle, left .

As was the case with Short Time Circuit and The Key-side Conundrum, a second player is very helpful for this puzzle.

  • Coordinates: Puzzle Room at 138, 512
  • Required Character: Any character

Mirror Mirth is found inside a Puzzle Room at 138, 512. If you aren't able to enter it, continue solving Riddler Puzzles across Gotham and wait for it to appear on the map.

Once you can go inside the Puzzle Room, take these actions to solve it:

  1. Grab the lamp on the table and place it on the pedestal furthest from the mirror.
  2. Collect the bear from the bookshelf and place it on the seat in front of the piano.
  3. Press the red button near the entrance to the room to roll up a dock door. Grab the candles from the ground beyond the dock door and put them on the table.
  4. Pick up the crank near the table, take it into the room where you found the candles, use it on the gears to lower the cage with the boombox, and place the boombox on the pedestal closest to the mirror.

Yes , activating The Cauldron North tower will reveal collectible and activity icons in the district, including Riddler Puzzles. However, finding a puzzle is just the beginning, as you must then solve it.

For more on map-reveal towers, see our All Towers & How to Access Them guide.

Do The Cauldron North Riddler Puzzles Count Toward 100% Completion?

Yes , you need to solve all seven The Cauldron North - Ace Chemicals Riddler Puzzles to earn the A Watchful Collector achievement and reach 100% completion.

item.title: Scratch to Riches Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
Scratch to Riches is a super exciting luck-based Roblox game where you buy scratchers, reveal awesome rewards like coins and boosts, then use them to unlock new stuff and become the richest player around.

Codes are a great way to get free rewards that help you level up faster and have even more fun scratching away. We've got the latest working codes for Scratch to Riches that you can grab right now simply by checking the box below!

All Scratch to Riches Codes

Working Scratch to Riches Codes

Code

Reward(s)

UPDATE1

Boosts

COIN

2000 Coins

COIN2

2000 Coins

COIN3

2000 Coins

BUGFIX

Boosts

BUGFIX2

Boosts

LUCKY1

1 Lucky Roll

LUCKY2

1 Lucky Roll

250KVISIT

20 Credits and Boosts

1KCCU

20 Credits and Boosts

Expired Scratch to Riches Codes

There are currently no expired codes for Scratch to Riches .

These working codes are awesome because they hand you free coins, XP boosts, money multipliers, and lucky rolls right away.

These codes allow you to buy better scratchers quicker, reveal bigger prizes, and race up the leaderboards without waiting forever.

How to Redeem Scratch to Riches Codes

  • First, open Scratch to Riches in Roblox .
  • Click the “I” button at the top right corner.
  • See the redemption section there and type or paste the code exactly as shown (no extra spaces).
  • Hit the redeem button and watch your rewards pop up instantly.

How to Get More Scratch to Riches Codes

The developers share new promo codes first in the official Scratch to Riches Discord server, so hop over there and turn on notifications for the latest drops. You can also watch for in-game announcements or group posts when big updates hit.

We update this article regularly with every fresh batch of working codes for this Roblox game, so the smartest move is to bookmark this page and swing by every few days. That way, you’ll always stay ahead and keep those free rewards rolling in without missing a single one!

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by

These working codes are awesome because they hand you free coins, XP boosts, money multipliers, and lucky rolls right away.

  • First, open Scratch to Riches in Roblox .
  • Click the “I” button at the top right corner.
  • See the redemption section there and type or paste the code exactly as shown (no extra spaces).
  • Hit the redeem button and watch your rewards pop up instantly.

The developers share new promo codes first in the official Scratch to Riches Discord server, so hop over there and turn on notifications for the latest drops. You can also watch for in-game announcements or group posts when big updates hit.

item.title: The Greatest Lord Of The Rings Games To Play In 2026
item.description:
Of all the iconic IPs out there, it's surprising how up and down the popularity of J.R.R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings has been. There is the incomparable movie trilogy directed by Peter Jackson, the decent but overstuffed Hobbit movie trilogy, and The Rings of Power TV show, which gets more hate than it deserves.

Then there are the video games based on The Lord of the Rings, which are also a mixed bag. While The Lord of the Rings: Gollum is the most recent disaster, there are still plenty of great Lord of the Rings games to play in 2026 while we wait for Warhorse Studios' upcoming LotR open-world game.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Middle-Earth: Shadow Of War

Glory To The Nemesis System

Middle-Earth: Shadow of War
9 /10
Released
October 10, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Intense Violence
Developer(s)
Monolith
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Powered by

Let's get the obvious one out of the way. As far as modern The Lord of the Rings games go, nothing holds a candle to Monolith Productions' Shadow duology. While Middle-earth: Shadow of War has its detractors, mostly due to the completely needless inclusion of microtransactions, it is still the best modern LotR game, and one of the best games based on Tolkien's work ever made.

This is largely due to the spectacular Nemesis System, which organically creates Orc generals and recurring enemies for you to face off against. Instead of a pre-determined "big bad," Shadow of War creates one based on the enemies who defeat you in combat, or who manage to survive one of your killing blows. It's an incredible mechanic that is sorely underused in the gaming world today.

The Lord Of The Rings: The Return Of The King

My Kingdom For A Remake

The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
Hack and Slash
Action
Systems
Released
October 31, 2003
ESRB
t

Developer(s)
EA Redwood Shores
Genre(s)
Hack and Slash, Action
Powered by

Very rarely will you see me (or anyone) advocating for a licensed movie tie-in game from the 2000s to get a wholesale remake for modern hardware, but there are exactly three exceptions to that rule: Spider-Man 2, and the tie-in games for The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers and The Return of the King. While both LotR games are good, The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King is simply a deeper game.

These aren't quite RPGs, although you do level up your characters as you progress. They're more like action games, with a roster of characters to play as and a host of unique levels to complete, each connecting to key moments from the movies. The Return of the King has more characters, levels, and even additional story beats that don't appear in the movies. They're also just a blast to play, with excellent combat and a ton of unique interactions for each level.

The Lord Of The Rings: Battle For Middle-Earth 2

Tolkien Meets Real-Time Strategy

The Lord of the Rings: The Battle for Middle-earth 2
Real-Time Strategy
Systems
Released
March 2, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
T // Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
EA Los Angeles
Genre(s)
Real-Time Strategy
Powered by

Real-time strategy and Middle-earth are an obvious pairing, and that's why the Battle for Middle-earth series, and particularly The Lord of the Rings: Battle for Middle-earth 2 , is so excellent. It captures the epic scale and stunning visuals of Tolkien's massive battles during the War of the Ring in a way that few other games can match.

What elevates the sequel above the first game is that it takes place in an often-overlooked portion of the war: the battles in the north. This adds a level of unpredictability in both the settings of each battle and the armies involved. Just like the first game, you can play as either good or evil armies. The good side is often on the defensive, forced to fight uphill battles and rely on clever tactics, while the evil side has superior numbers and can revel in the destruction of iconic locations like Rivendell or the Shire.

Tales Of The Shire: A The Lord Of The Rings Game

Now We're Cooking

Tales of the Shire: A The Lord of the Rings Game
7 /10
Released
July 29, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Wētā Workshop
Genre(s)
Life Simulation, RPG, Adventure
Powered by

Not every Lord of the Rings game needs to be about large-scale battles and the fate of the world. In Tales of the Shire, you play a Hobbit starting a new home in the village (or not, depending on which Hobbit you ask) of Bywater. It's a cozy life-sim game that's light on narrative and more interested in a slower-paced experience full of idyllic moments and casual chats.

Central to all this is an impressively complex cooking system, which is what the game's social elements revolve around. You spend your day inviting other Hobbits to dinner, and when they RSVP, they'll tell you what they'd like to eat. Then, it's up to you to prepare those meals. That preparation involves everything from finding and chopping ingredients to cooking to seasoning. It's a low-stakes game meant to be a casual, relaxing experience, but isn't that what life as a Hobbit is all about?

LEGO The Lord Of The Rings: The Video Game

Gondor Never Looked This Blocky

LEGO The Lord of the Rings: The Video Game
Released
October 30, 2012

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
E10+ // Cartoon Violence, Comic Mischief
Developer(s)
Traveller's Tales, TT Fusion
Genre(s)
Action-Adventure
Powered by

If the virtual Tolkien experience you're looking for involves nothing more than a playable version of the key moments in each of the three Lord of the Rings movies, then LEGO The Lord of the Rings: The Video Game is your best bet. Much like other licensed LEGO video games, this one isn't reinventing the wheel here. It follows the movies to a T, with a healthy helping of patented LEGO humor thrown in for good measure.

The most impressive thing about LEGO The Lord of the Rings is that it is entirely open world. Even though the game's missions will lead you on a relatively linear path, you're free to set off in any direction you like, encountering dozens of famous Middle-earth landmarks along the way. Moreover, characters who never appear in the movies but are key parts of Tolkien's original novels are available as unlockable characters, providing a more complete experience in some ways, while the game might skip over less-important story beats in others. It's a fair compromise, and one that's sure to please any LotR fan.

The Lord Of The Rings Online

One Of The Most Underrated MMOs

The Lord of the Rings Online
Released
April 24, 2007

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Blood and Gore, Use of Alcohol and Tobacco, Violence
Developer(s)
Standing Stone Games
Genre(s)
MMORPG
Powered by

MMOs have come a long way since the late 2000s, but if you're still comfortable with the gameplay loop of titles like WoW Classic or Guild Wars 2, then The Lord of the Rings Online is one of the most underrated entries in the genre, even for non- LotR fans. Sure, its combat and character creator are dated by today's standards. However, where this game excels, it rivals (and even exceeds) the best MMOs out there.

Let's start with the world and quest design. When it comes to a game that lets you immerse yourself in Middle-earth and experience it in all its glory, without the need for constant action or drama, The Lord of the Rings Online is second to none, especially if you start with the free-to-play Before the Shadow DLC, which is designed to welcome newcomers to the game. The writing and quest design here are of an exceptional quality, and if you're coming in as a LotR fan, they will blow you away. Peter Jackson's movie trilogy is obviously a spectacular adaptation of Tolkien's work, but The Lord of the Rings Online, in many ways, stands shoulder to shoulder with those classic films. It's really that good.

The Lord Of The Rings: War In The North

LotR As An Action RPG

The Lord of the Rings: War in the North
Action RPG
Systems
7 /10
Released
November 1, 2011
ESRB
Mature 17+ // Blood and Gore, Intense Violence

Developer(s)
Snowblind Studios
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Powered by

There are surprisingly few action RPGs based on The Lord of the Rings. We have Monolith's Shadow of Mordor and Shadow of War, but those are more open-world action games than anything, without much in the way of RPG elements. Then there's The Lord of the Rings: The Third Age, which is an unbalanced mess, albeit a creative one. Often overlooked in this conversation is The Lord of the Rings: War in the North, which is, in some ways, the best of the bunch.

Combat here is especially brutal (which earned the game its M rating), with orc dismemberments and beheadings aplenty. The narrative, once again, explores the northern campaign of the War of the Ring, this time telling a completely original tale that runs parallel to, and sometimes intersects with, the original trilogy. War in the North isn't going to change how you look at RPGs or anything, but for seasoned LotR fans looking for something new, this game will feel like a breath of fresh air.

The Lord Of The Rings: Conquest

Star Wars: Battlefront Set In Middle-Earth

The Lord of the Rings: Conquest
Released
January 13, 2009

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
T // Violence
Developer(s)
Pandemic Studios
Genre(s)
Action
Powered by

One wonders why there were never many attempts to emulate the success of Star Wars: Battlefront, given the relative simplicity of the concept. However, one game that tried to carry that torch was The Lord of the Rings: Conquest. It follows the formula you would expect. You play as the heroic side first, choosing from one of four classes and participating in some of the series' most iconic battles, while occasionally jumping into the fray as a recognizable hero character.

Subscribe for deep coverage of Lord of the Rings games

Discover curated insight into Lord of the Rings games: subscribe to the newsletter for thoughtful reviews, hidden gems, and context to help you choose which Middle-earth titles to play, plus broader gaming coverage that complements these deep dives.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

However, the real twist comes when you complete the heroic campaign, which aligns with the moment when Frodo destroys the Ring. Then, you unlock the evil campaign, which follows a narrative where Frodo kept the Ring instead. This is by far the more interesting campaign of the two, featuring shocking narrative moments that might feel like blasphemy to Tolkien purists. Most importantly, The Lord of the Rings: Conquest is playable in co-op, and is actually much better that way than playing solo. If you've got some friends and a PS3 on hand, it's absolutely worth a look.
item.content:encoded:
Of all the iconic IPs out there, it's surprising how up and down the popularity of J.R.R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings has been. There is the incomparable movie trilogy directed by Peter Jackson, the decent but overstuffed Hobbit movie trilogy, and The Rings of Power TV show, which gets more hate than it deserves.

Then there are the video games based on The Lord of the Rings, which are also a mixed bag. While The Lord of the Rings: Gollum is the most recent disaster, there are still plenty of great Lord of the Rings games to play in 2026 while we wait for Warhorse Studios' upcoming LotR open-world game.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Middle-Earth: Shadow Of War

Glory To The Nemesis System

Middle-Earth: Shadow of War
9 /10
Released
October 10, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Intense Violence
Developer(s)
Monolith
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Powered by

Let's get the obvious one out of the way. As far as modern The Lord of the Rings games go, nothing holds a candle to Monolith Productions' Shadow duology. While Middle-earth: Shadow of War has its detractors, mostly due to the completely needless inclusion of microtransactions, it is still the best modern LotR game, and one of the best games based on Tolkien's work ever made.

This is largely due to the spectacular Nemesis System, which organically creates Orc generals and recurring enemies for you to face off against. Instead of a pre-determined "big bad," Shadow of War creates one based on the enemies who defeat you in combat, or who manage to survive one of your killing blows. It's an incredible mechanic that is sorely underused in the gaming world today.

The Lord Of The Rings: The Return Of The King

My Kingdom For A Remake

The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
Hack and Slash
Action
Systems
Released
October 31, 2003
ESRB
t

Developer(s)
EA Redwood Shores
Genre(s)
Hack and Slash, Action
Powered by

Very rarely will you see me (or anyone) advocating for a licensed movie tie-in game from the 2000s to get a wholesale remake for modern hardware, but there are exactly three exceptions to that rule: Spider-Man 2, and the tie-in games for The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers and The Return of the King. While both LotR games are good, The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King is simply a deeper game.

These aren't quite RPGs, although you do level up your characters as you progress. They're more like action games, with a roster of characters to play as and a host of unique levels to complete, each connecting to key moments from the movies. The Return of the King has more characters, levels, and even additional story beats that don't appear in the movies. They're also just a blast to play, with excellent combat and a ton of unique interactions for each level.

The Lord Of The Rings: Battle For Middle-Earth 2

Tolkien Meets Real-Time Strategy

The Lord of the Rings: The Battle for Middle-earth 2
Real-Time Strategy
Systems
Released
March 2, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
T // Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
EA Los Angeles
Genre(s)
Real-Time Strategy
Powered by

Real-time strategy and Middle-earth are an obvious pairing, and that's why the Battle for Middle-earth series, and particularly The Lord of the Rings: Battle for Middle-earth 2 , is so excellent. It captures the epic scale and stunning visuals of Tolkien's massive battles during the War of the Ring in a way that few other games can match.

What elevates the sequel above the first game is that it takes place in an often-overlooked portion of the war: the battles in the north. This adds a level of unpredictability in both the settings of each battle and the armies involved. Just like the first game, you can play as either good or evil armies. The good side is often on the defensive, forced to fight uphill battles and rely on clever tactics, while the evil side has superior numbers and can revel in the destruction of iconic locations like Rivendell or the Shire.

Tales Of The Shire: A The Lord Of The Rings Game

Now We're Cooking

Tales of the Shire: A The Lord of the Rings Game
7 /10
Released
July 29, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Wētā Workshop
Genre(s)
Life Simulation, RPG, Adventure
Powered by

Not every Lord of the Rings game needs to be about large-scale battles and the fate of the world. In Tales of the Shire, you play a Hobbit starting a new home in the village (or not, depending on which Hobbit you ask) of Bywater. It's a cozy life-sim game that's light on narrative and more interested in a slower-paced experience full of idyllic moments and casual chats.

Central to all this is an impressively complex cooking system, which is what the game's social elements revolve around. You spend your day inviting other Hobbits to dinner, and when they RSVP, they'll tell you what they'd like to eat. Then, it's up to you to prepare those meals. That preparation involves everything from finding and chopping ingredients to cooking to seasoning. It's a low-stakes game meant to be a casual, relaxing experience, but isn't that what life as a Hobbit is all about?

LEGO The Lord Of The Rings: The Video Game

Gondor Never Looked This Blocky

LEGO The Lord of the Rings: The Video Game
Released
October 30, 2012

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
E10+ // Cartoon Violence, Comic Mischief
Developer(s)
Traveller's Tales, TT Fusion
Genre(s)
Action-Adventure
Powered by

If the virtual Tolkien experience you're looking for involves nothing more than a playable version of the key moments in each of the three Lord of the Rings movies, then LEGO The Lord of the Rings: The Video Game is your best bet. Much like other licensed LEGO video games, this one isn't reinventing the wheel here. It follows the movies to a T, with a healthy helping of patented LEGO humor thrown in for good measure.

The most impressive thing about LEGO The Lord of the Rings is that it is entirely open world. Even though the game's missions will lead you on a relatively linear path, you're free to set off in any direction you like, encountering dozens of famous Middle-earth landmarks along the way. Moreover, characters who never appear in the movies but are key parts of Tolkien's original novels are available as unlockable characters, providing a more complete experience in some ways, while the game might skip over less-important story beats in others. It's a fair compromise, and one that's sure to please any LotR fan.

The Lord Of The Rings Online

One Of The Most Underrated MMOs

The Lord of the Rings Online
Released
April 24, 2007

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Blood and Gore, Use of Alcohol and Tobacco, Violence
Developer(s)
Standing Stone Games
Genre(s)
MMORPG
Powered by

MMOs have come a long way since the late 2000s, but if you're still comfortable with the gameplay loop of titles like WoW Classic or Guild Wars 2, then The Lord of the Rings Online is one of the most underrated entries in the genre, even for non- LotR fans. Sure, its combat and character creator are dated by today's standards. However, where this game excels, it rivals (and even exceeds) the best MMOs out there.

Let's start with the world and quest design. When it comes to a game that lets you immerse yourself in Middle-earth and experience it in all its glory, without the need for constant action or drama, The Lord of the Rings Online is second to none, especially if you start with the free-to-play Before the Shadow DLC, which is designed to welcome newcomers to the game. The writing and quest design here are of an exceptional quality, and if you're coming in as a LotR fan, they will blow you away. Peter Jackson's movie trilogy is obviously a spectacular adaptation of Tolkien's work, but The Lord of the Rings Online, in many ways, stands shoulder to shoulder with those classic films. It's really that good.

The Lord Of The Rings: War In The North

LotR As An Action RPG

The Lord of the Rings: War in the North
Action RPG
Systems
7 /10
Released
November 1, 2011
ESRB
Mature 17+ // Blood and Gore, Intense Violence

Developer(s)
Snowblind Studios
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Powered by

There are surprisingly few action RPGs based on The Lord of the Rings. We have Monolith's Shadow of Mordor and Shadow of War, but those are more open-world action games than anything, without much in the way of RPG elements. Then there's The Lord of the Rings: The Third Age, which is an unbalanced mess, albeit a creative one. Often overlooked in this conversation is The Lord of the Rings: War in the North, which is, in some ways, the best of the bunch.

Combat here is especially brutal (which earned the game its M rating), with orc dismemberments and beheadings aplenty. The narrative, once again, explores the northern campaign of the War of the Ring, this time telling a completely original tale that runs parallel to, and sometimes intersects with, the original trilogy. War in the North isn't going to change how you look at RPGs or anything, but for seasoned LotR fans looking for something new, this game will feel like a breath of fresh air.

The Lord Of The Rings: Conquest

Star Wars: Battlefront Set In Middle-Earth

The Lord of the Rings: Conquest
Released
January 13, 2009

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
T // Violence
Developer(s)
Pandemic Studios
Genre(s)
Action
Powered by

One wonders why there were never many attempts to emulate the success of Star Wars: Battlefront, given the relative simplicity of the concept. However, one game that tried to carry that torch was The Lord of the Rings: Conquest. It follows the formula you would expect. You play as the heroic side first, choosing from one of four classes and participating in some of the series' most iconic battles, while occasionally jumping into the fray as a recognizable hero character.

Subscribe for deep coverage of Lord of the Rings games

Discover curated insight into Lord of the Rings games: subscribe to the newsletter for thoughtful reviews, hidden gems, and context to help you choose which Middle-earth titles to play, plus broader gaming coverage that complements these deep dives.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

However, the real twist comes when you complete the heroic campaign, which aligns with the moment when Frodo destroys the Ring. Then, you unlock the evil campaign, which follows a narrative where Frodo kept the Ring instead. This is by far the more interesting campaign of the two, featuring shocking narrative moments that might feel like blasphemy to Tolkien purists. Most importantly, The Lord of the Rings: Conquest is playable in co-op, and is actually much better that way than playing solo. If you've got some friends and a PS3 on hand, it's absolutely worth a look.

item.title: The 5 Most Corrupt & Evil Governments In Video Game History
item.description:
There's no trope quite like the corrupt government. They're easy to hate, guaranteed to make you root against them, and their downfall is both satisfying and inspiring. It's the kind of thing that humans have been writing about for centuries, and video games have taken up that torch and carried on the tradition.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

In today's world, we can see that kind of corruption at all levels of government, no matter where we live. Some get more media attention than others, and some are far more oppressive, but no country is wholly safe from corrupt leadership. If you want a glimpse at just how bad things can get, but don't want to doomscroll to get it, these next games feature some of the most evil governments we've ever seen. Hopefully, by playing them, you can bring about their downfall.

Arstotzka - Papers, Please

The Other Is Dangerous, But We Will Keep You Safe

Papers, Please
9 /10
Released
August 8, 2013

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
M For Mature 17+ Due To Violence, Blood, Sexual Themes, Nudity, Drug Reference, Strong Language
Developer(s)
3909 LLC
Genre(s)
Simulation
Powered by

Maybe the most familiar of any government on this list, Arstotzka is a communist dictatorship where forced labor is commonplace. Citizens of Arstotzka aren't even allowed to work if they aren't also building a family, as they are expected to continue the country's growth in order to live there.

Working as an inspector at the border checkpoint between Arstotzka and Kolechia, you get to see first-hand the tyrannical overreach of the government, all done under the guise of protecting the homeland. Racial profiling, invasive searches, and detached cruelty are all job expectations. People will come to your booth and beg you for help, desperate and starving, but if you help them, your pay will be docked, which means your family will suffer. You have no rights in this country. Toe the line, follow orders, and don't trust outsiders. Glory to Arstotzka.

Shinra - Final Fantasy 7

The Company And The Government Are One And The Same

Final Fantasy 7
Released
January 31, 1997

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T for Teen: Blood, Fantasy Violence, Language, Mild Suggestive Themes
Developer(s)
Square Enix
Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

You might be looking at this and thinking: "Wait, Shinra is a company, not a government." While you're technically right, the company controls the electricity for all of Midgar, putting them in a position of power that dominates the "official" politicians, including Mayor Domino. President Shinra, the head of Shinra Electric Power Company, is also a politician (sound familiar?), and his plans for Midgar and the Mako that resides underneath it have no room for local politics to get in the way. He even started and won a war with the Republic of Junon, conquering the country and subjugating its populace by forcing them to live beneath his new megacity without food or water.

The resistance group Avalanche (or terrorist group, according to Shinra) has already caught wind of this, and in Final Fantasy 7 we pick up their first foray in taking the fight to the megacorporation. Just look at what Shinra throws back at them. Private militia forces, super soldiers, giant security robots, dangerous experiments, and more. Shinra may be "just" a corporation in name, but its actions show that it has far more control than any actual government, and it wields that control to further President Shinra's ambitions and nothing more.

The Conglomerate - Mirror's Edge

Knowledge Is Dangerous

Mirror's Edge
Released
November 11, 2008

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Blood, Language, Violence
Developer(s)
DICE
Genre(s)
Action, Platformer
Powered by

The Conglomerate is another megacorporation-turned-ruling power, but in this case, they aren't subtle about it. Several powerful and wealthy families, known as the Corporate Houses, gradually took over the government of The City and then expanded to conquer all of Cascadia, toppling the reigning communist regime in the process. The people did rise up in an event known as the November Riots, but these were deemed to be violent and were quashed by the City Protection Force. Today, automated security systems monitor all activity on the streets, and personalized tracking bracelets keep data on everyone's identity and financial information.

This surveillance is largely designed to crack down on the spread of information. If the Conglomerate controls what information its people have access to, it can control their understanding of their own subjugation. That's where Runners like Faith, the protagonist of Mirror's Edge, operate. They take to the rooftops where surveillance is light and run across the city to deliver illicit information to the populace. Just look at what Runners have to accomplish to pull off their work, and you start to understand how oppressive the regime is on the ground, even if you never see it for yourself.

The Combine - Half-Life 2

Submit To Your Alien Rulers

Half-Life 2
Released
November 16, 2004

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Intense Violence
Developer(s)
Valve
Genre(s)
Shooter
Powered by

Most people don't even realize this after playing Half-Life 2, but the Combine are not the soldiers we see patrolling City 17. Those are the humans already subjugated by The Combine; the rulers themselves are something greater, more insidious, and almost completely unknowable. They are an interdimensional alien force comprised of many alien races, some allied and others enslaved. Their only goal is expansion and perfection across both space and dimensions.

The Combine conquered humanity in what is known as the Seven-Hour War, and that name tells you just how futile Earth's resistance was against them. Dr. Wallace Breen was nominated as humanity's representative, and he argued that humans were more valuable alive than eradicated. That's the only reason the human race still exists in Half-Life 2. The Combine is ruthless in its subjugation, eradicating human identity through experiments and invasive surgeries in an attempt to forcibly assimilate the entire race. There is no local justification behind the Combine's rule, false or otherwise; they simply dominate, assimilate, and move on in a constant search for total power and control.

Alpha Section - Beyond Good & Evil

Join Your Liberators, Protect Hillys, Become A Hero

Beyond Good And Evil
Released
November 11, 2003

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Comic Mischief, Violence
Developer(s)
Ubisoft Milan, Ubisoft Montpellier
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

Alpha Section is the classic totalitarian government that presents itself as a hero and liberator to stifle any dissent among the populace about its actions. During a failing war against the DomZ, a parasitic alien race, a mercenary group called Alpha Section was hired to bolster the Hillyan military. This eventually turned the tide of the war. However, it was later revealed that Alpha Section didn't so much bolster the Hillyan forces as it did take command of them.

Once they had reduced the military's influence, Alpha Section quickly took backdoor control of the Hillyan government and began a massive propaganda campaign proclaiming themselves as war heroes and undertaking a massive recruitment effort. It's later revealed that their recruits were then subjected to bioengineering experiments using DomZ technology. Alpha Section also engaged in kidnapping and human trafficking to further expand its ranks. In the finale of Beyond Good and Evil, Jade discovers that Alpha Section was, in fact, colluding with the DomZ in an effort to overthrow the Hillyan government. After Jade and IRIS broadcast this information to the people of Hillys, the military turns on Alpha Section, chasing them off of their moon base and restoring government control for the time being.
item.content:encoded:
There's no trope quite like the corrupt government. They're easy to hate, guaranteed to make you root against them, and their downfall is both satisfying and inspiring. It's the kind of thing that humans have been writing about for centuries, and video games have taken up that torch and carried on the tradition.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

In today's world, we can see that kind of corruption at all levels of government, no matter where we live. Some get more media attention than others, and some are far more oppressive, but no country is wholly safe from corrupt leadership. If you want a glimpse at just how bad things can get, but don't want to doomscroll to get it, these next games feature some of the most evil governments we've ever seen. Hopefully, by playing them, you can bring about their downfall.

Arstotzka - Papers, Please

The Other Is Dangerous, But We Will Keep You Safe

Papers, Please
9 /10
Released
August 8, 2013

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
M For Mature 17+ Due To Violence, Blood, Sexual Themes, Nudity, Drug Reference, Strong Language
Developer(s)
3909 LLC
Genre(s)
Simulation
Powered by

Maybe the most familiar of any government on this list, Arstotzka is a communist dictatorship where forced labor is commonplace. Citizens of Arstotzka aren't even allowed to work if they aren't also building a family, as they are expected to continue the country's growth in order to live there.

Working as an inspector at the border checkpoint between Arstotzka and Kolechia, you get to see first-hand the tyrannical overreach of the government, all done under the guise of protecting the homeland. Racial profiling, invasive searches, and detached cruelty are all job expectations. People will come to your booth and beg you for help, desperate and starving, but if you help them, your pay will be docked, which means your family will suffer. You have no rights in this country. Toe the line, follow orders, and don't trust outsiders. Glory to Arstotzka.

Shinra - Final Fantasy 7

The Company And The Government Are One And The Same

Final Fantasy 7
Released
January 31, 1997

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T for Teen: Blood, Fantasy Violence, Language, Mild Suggestive Themes
Developer(s)
Square Enix
Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

You might be looking at this and thinking: "Wait, Shinra is a company, not a government." While you're technically right, the company controls the electricity for all of Midgar, putting them in a position of power that dominates the "official" politicians, including Mayor Domino. President Shinra, the head of Shinra Electric Power Company, is also a politician (sound familiar?), and his plans for Midgar and the Mako that resides underneath it have no room for local politics to get in the way. He even started and won a war with the Republic of Junon, conquering the country and subjugating its populace by forcing them to live beneath his new megacity without food or water.

The resistance group Avalanche (or terrorist group, according to Shinra) has already caught wind of this, and in Final Fantasy 7 we pick up their first foray in taking the fight to the megacorporation. Just look at what Shinra throws back at them. Private militia forces, super soldiers, giant security robots, dangerous experiments, and more. Shinra may be "just" a corporation in name, but its actions show that it has far more control than any actual government, and it wields that control to further President Shinra's ambitions and nothing more.

The Conglomerate - Mirror's Edge

Knowledge Is Dangerous

Mirror's Edge
Released
November 11, 2008

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Blood, Language, Violence
Developer(s)
DICE
Genre(s)
Action, Platformer
Powered by

The Conglomerate is another megacorporation-turned-ruling power, but in this case, they aren't subtle about it. Several powerful and wealthy families, known as the Corporate Houses, gradually took over the government of The City and then expanded to conquer all of Cascadia, toppling the reigning communist regime in the process. The people did rise up in an event known as the November Riots, but these were deemed to be violent and were quashed by the City Protection Force. Today, automated security systems monitor all activity on the streets, and personalized tracking bracelets keep data on everyone's identity and financial information.

This surveillance is largely designed to crack down on the spread of information. If the Conglomerate controls what information its people have access to, it can control their understanding of their own subjugation. That's where Runners like Faith, the protagonist of Mirror's Edge, operate. They take to the rooftops where surveillance is light and run across the city to deliver illicit information to the populace. Just look at what Runners have to accomplish to pull off their work, and you start to understand how oppressive the regime is on the ground, even if you never see it for yourself.

The Combine - Half-Life 2

Submit To Your Alien Rulers

Half-Life 2
Released
November 16, 2004

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Intense Violence
Developer(s)
Valve
Genre(s)
Shooter
Powered by

Most people don't even realize this after playing Half-Life 2, but the Combine are not the soldiers we see patrolling City 17. Those are the humans already subjugated by The Combine; the rulers themselves are something greater, more insidious, and almost completely unknowable. They are an interdimensional alien force comprised of many alien races, some allied and others enslaved. Their only goal is expansion and perfection across both space and dimensions.

The Combine conquered humanity in what is known as the Seven-Hour War, and that name tells you just how futile Earth's resistance was against them. Dr. Wallace Breen was nominated as humanity's representative, and he argued that humans were more valuable alive than eradicated. That's the only reason the human race still exists in Half-Life 2. The Combine is ruthless in its subjugation, eradicating human identity through experiments and invasive surgeries in an attempt to forcibly assimilate the entire race. There is no local justification behind the Combine's rule, false or otherwise; they simply dominate, assimilate, and move on in a constant search for total power and control.

Alpha Section - Beyond Good & Evil

Join Your Liberators, Protect Hillys, Become A Hero

Beyond Good And Evil
Released
November 11, 2003

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T For Teen due to Comic Mischief, Violence
Developer(s)
Ubisoft Milan, Ubisoft Montpellier
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

Alpha Section is the classic totalitarian government that presents itself as a hero and liberator to stifle any dissent among the populace about its actions. During a failing war against the DomZ, a parasitic alien race, a mercenary group called Alpha Section was hired to bolster the Hillyan military. This eventually turned the tide of the war. However, it was later revealed that Alpha Section didn't so much bolster the Hillyan forces as it did take command of them.

Once they had reduced the military's influence, Alpha Section quickly took backdoor control of the Hillyan government and began a massive propaganda campaign proclaiming themselves as war heroes and undertaking a massive recruitment effort. It's later revealed that their recruits were then subjected to bioengineering experiments using DomZ technology. Alpha Section also engaged in kidnapping and human trafficking to further expand its ranks. In the finale of Beyond Good and Evil, Jade discovers that Alpha Section was, in fact, colluding with the DomZ in an effort to overthrow the Hillyan government. After Jade and IRIS broadcast this information to the people of Hillys, the military turns on Alpha Section, chasing them off of their moon base and restoring government control for the time being.
